# Let's implement normalization functions and search the hadith DB for close matches.
import re, unicodedata, math, sys, collections, heapq

# load hadith list already loaded as hadith variable
# target strings
full_span = "لَا يَلْبَسِ الْحَرِيرَ إِلَّا مَنْ لَا طَاقَةَ لَهُ بِهِ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ"
snippet = "لَا يَلْبَسِ الْحَرِيرَ إِلَّا مَنْ لَا طَاقَةَ لَهُ بِهِ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ"  # same here

# normalization
arabic_diacritics = re.compile(r'[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u06D6-\u06ED]')
tatweel = '\u0640'  # 'ـ'
def normalize_ar(text):
    # remove tashkeel & tatweel
    text = text.replace(tatweel, '')
    text = arabic_diacritics.sub('', text)
    # remove punctuation, unify hamza forms: إ أ آ ٱ ء ئ ؤ -> ا or maybe remove maddah
    # map hamzas to bare ا where possible, replace ALEF MAKSURA with ي, DOTLESS YA? We'll do common mapping:
    mapping = {
        'أ':'ا','إ':'ا','آ':'ا','ٱ':'ا',
        'ى':'ي','ئ':'ي','ؤ':'و','ء':'', 'ٮ':''
    }
    for k,v in mapping.items():
        text = text.replace(k,v)
    # remove punctuation and punctuation like quotes, commas, brackets, numbers, latin etc
    text = re.sub(r'[^\u0600-\u06FF\s]', ' ', text)  # keep Arabic letters and spaces
    # collapse whitespace and lowercase (no lowercase in Arabic)
    text = re.sub(r'\s+',' ', text).strip()
    return text

norm_full = normalize_ar(full_span)
norm_full[:200], norm_full, len(norm_full)

# Let's search for candidates with token overlap and LCS ratio.
def tokens(text):
    return text.split()

def token_f1(a,b):
    # a,b normalized strings
    ta = a.split()
    tb = b.split()
    sa = collections.Counter(ta)
    sb = collections.Counter(tb)
    common = sum(min(sa[w], sb[w]) for w in set(sa)&set(sb))
    if common == 0:
        return 0.0
    prec = common / len(tb)  # how much of candidate is in query? Hmm
    rec = common / len(ta)
    if prec+rec == 0:
        return 0.0
    return 2*prec*rec/(prec+rec)

def lcs_length(a,b):
    # normalized lcs length using DP on characters (may be heavy)
    n,m = len(a), len(b)
    # if long, we can compute only for those with token overlap > 0
    dp = [0]*(m+1)
    for i in range(1,n+1):
        prev = 0
        ai = a[i-1]
        for j in range(1,m+1):
            temp = dp[j]
            if ai == b[j-1]:
                dp[j] = prev + 1
            else:
                if dp[j] < dp[j-1]:
                    dp[j] = dp[j-1]
            prev = temp
    return dp[m]

# Let's iterate and compute scores
norm_query = norm_full
best = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(hadith):
    # select textual field
    text = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ''
    if not text:
        continue
    norm_text = normalize_ar(text)
    # compute token f1
    f1 = token_f1(norm_text, norm_query)
    # quick filter: if token overlap > 0
    if f1 > 0.1:
        # compute lcs ratio char-level between normalized strings
        # To speed, we can compute longest common substring? But lcs (subsequence) maybe acceptable.
        lcs_len = lcs_length(norm_text, norm_query)
        lcs_ratio = lcs_len / max(len(norm_query),1)
        # store
        best.append((f1, lcs_ratio, idx, text))
# sort by f1 then lcs
best_sorted = sorted(best, key=lambda x:(-x[0], -x[1]))
best_sorted[:20], len(best_sorted)

# print the full entry for index 20402 (if exists)
idx = 20402
print(hadith[idx])

# Recompute token_f1 for all entries and pick top 100 by token f1 then compute Levenshtein ratio
def levenshtein(a,b):
    n,m=len(a),len(b)
    if n==0: return m
    if m==0: return n
    prev = list(range(m+1))
    for i in range(1,n+1):
        curr = [i] + [0]*m
        ai = a[i-1]
        for j in range(1,m+1):
            cost = 0 if ai==b[j-1] else 1
            curr[j] = min(prev[j]+1, curr[j-1]+1, prev[j-1]+cost)
        prev = curr
    return prev[m]

# compute token f1 for all hadith entries
scores = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(hadith):
    text = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ''
    if not text: continue
    norm_text = normalize_ar(text)
    f1 = token_f1(norm_text, norm_full)
    if f1>0:
        scores.append((f1, idx, norm_text, text))
# top 200
top = sorted(scores, key=lambda x: -x[0])[:200]
# compute Levenshtein ratio and print top by levenshtein
lev_results = []
for f1, idx, norm_text, text in top:
    ed = levenshtein(norm_text, norm_full)
    maxlen = max(len(norm_text), len(norm_full))
    ratio = 1 - ed/maxlen
    lev_results.append((ratio, f1, idx, text, norm_text))
lev_results_sorted = sorted(lev_results, key=lambda x:-x[0])
lev_results_sorted[:30]
